eel: noun. Any freshwater fish of the order Anguilliformes, which are elongated and resemble snakes.
else: adjective. Other; in addition to previously mentioned items.
ess: noun. The name of the Latin script letter S / s.
heel: noun. The rear part of the foot, where it joins the leg.
hell: noun. A place or situation of great suffering in life.
help: noun. Action given to provide assistance; aid.
helps: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of help
lee: noun. A protected cove or harbor, out of the wind.
lees: noun. The sediment that settles during fermentation of beverages, consisting of dead yeast and precipitated parts of the fruit.
peel: verb. To remove the skin or outer covering of.
peh: noun. Alternative form of pe (“Semitic letter”).
pes: noun. the foot of a human
see: verb. To perceive or detect someone or something with the eyes, or as if by sight.
seels: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of seel
seep: verb. To ooze or pass slowly through pores or other small openings, and in overly small quantities; said of liquids, etc.
sees: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of see
sel: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Selkup.
sell: verb. To transfer goods or provide services in exchange for money.
sells: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of sell
she: pronoun. The female (typically) person or animal previously mentioned or implied.
sheep: noun. A woolly ruminant of the genus Ovis.
shell: noun. A hard external covering of an animal.
sleep: verb. To rest in a state of reduced consciousness.
spell: noun. Words or a formula supposed to have magical powers.
heles: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of hele
hesp: noun. A measure of two hanks of linen thread.
lese: verb. Alternative form of leese (“to lose”)
shp: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Shipibo-Conibo.
sps: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Saposa.
ssh: interjection. Be quiet or reduce the volume.
